purpose: The commit addresses an issue where commands were being queued even after the HCI_UNREGISTER flag was set, which could lead to a use-after-free (UAF) error. The fix ensures that hci_cmd_sync_queue returns an error in such cases to prevent this issue.
The commit removes an unnecessary include statement in n_hdlc.c to clean up the codebase.
The commit updates documentation to reflect an 'N/A' metric for store operations in the perf c2c report table.

The commit addresses issues in vma lock handling within the hugetlb subsystem. It fixes race conditions during vma splitting and ensures proper lock management to prevent bugs like double freeing. The changes involve updating vm_ops to allocate new locks for split vmas and modifying how VM_MAYSHARE is handled to ensure correct lock presence.

The commit updates the device tree bindings for the rt5682s codec by documenting the AVDD and MICVDD supplies that are already in use. This ensures proper documentation without introducing new functionality or fixing existing issues.
The commit fixes a resource leak in the zorro7xx SCSI driver by adding an iounmap() call in the remove function to properly clean up resources.
The commit removes an unused work field from struct mlx5e_async_ctx, which is a code cleanup task.
The commit changes the type of a struct member from unsigned to signed to prevent warnings from static checkers when the key is NULL.
The commit sorts the CONFIG_SURFACE_PLATFORMS configuration without changing functionality, improving consistency.

The commit introduces a new function mcp251xfd_is_fd_mode() to replace an open-coded check for CAN-FD mode configuration in the chip's FIFOs. This change aims to improve code readability and maintainability by encapsulating the logic into a dedicated function.
The commit modifies selftests related to the RTC subsystem to skip when the RTC hardware is not present, preventing test failures in such cases.

The commit removes an unnecessary assignment of the variable 'bytes' in the ALSA echoaudio driver. This redundant assignment was causing a clang scan build warning and had no effect on the program's functionality.

The commit modifies the kernel configuration to allow rcuscale without requiring RCU Tasks when CONFIG_PREEMPT_NONE is enabled. This change reduces unnecessary kernel size and complexity by decoupling these components.
This commit removes a duplicated phrase 'be split' in the spte.h file within the KVM x86 MMU code. The change corrects a typo or redundancy in the source code comments.

The commit reverts a previous change in the drm/amd/display driver related to populating the dcn315 clk table based on dcfclk. This was done because using dcfclk-based values caused issues when certain states were disabled, leading to underflow situations.
The commit adds a compatible string to document the RZ/V2L SoC's Bifrost Mali-G31 GPU in device tree bindings, enabling kernel support for this hardware.
The commit renames struct pcie_port to dw_pcie_rp for consistency with other names in the DW PCIe core driver.
The commit addresses a clang -Wformat warning in the xgbe driver by correcting the format specifier to use %x instead of %hx, ensuring proper handling of integer promotions.

The commit fixes a bug in the MIPS architecture's jump_label code by adjusting how branch ranges are checked. It casts the upper bound to a long type to perform a correct signed comparison, preventing negative offsets from triggering warnings or errors.
The commit removes a specific case from SetHwReg8188EU() by directly calling the relevant function, part of efforts to eliminate the hal layer.
The commit addresses sparse warnings by declaring non-global symbols as static in the arm64 kernel code.
The commit removes incorrect interrupt flags from MDSS nodes in device tree bindings to fix configuration issues.
The commit modifies memory allocation in struct ctrl_ref to prepare for dynamic array size adjustments by drivers.
The commit adds support for additional PMICs and SoCs (SM8350, SC8280XP, SA8540P, SM8450) to the socinfo driver, expanding its hardware recognition capabilities.

The commit updates the Wi-Fi driver rtl8xxxu to recognize all possible chip versions (cuts) from A to P. Previously, it only recognized up to E and printed 'unknown' for higher versions. This change improves handling of different chip revisions.
The commit removes redundant software enable count management for PRCMU clocks in the ux500 device, as the core already handles this functionality.
The commit aims to refactor and clean up code by reducing #ifdef CONFIG_XIP_KERNEL usage in RISC-V mm init. It groups similar cases together to minimize conditional statements.

The commit enables QUP and GPI DMA hardware in the device tree for the sm6350-lena platform. This change prepares the system to support I2C nodes by ensuring necessary hardware components are enabled.
The commit improves code comments by clarifying terms, fixing typos, and adhering to coding style guidelines.
The commit addresses an issue where failing to acquire a hardware semaphore during resource release could cause an infinite loop. It adds error logging and exits the process to prevent this.
The commit updates the pcm3168a driver to use modern ASoC DAI format terminology, replacing old-style defines for clocking. This is a codebase improvement focusing on using current standards.
The commit removes unnecessary code related to IS_SWAPFILE in NFS, improving code quality by eliminating redundant checks.
The commit implements handling of GTP echo responses to comply with GTP specifications, preventing potential issues when Echo Requests are not handled.
The commit replaces certain cpumask functions with more appropriate ones to improve effectiveness. It also introduces a new function where needed.
The commit removes a call to 'dtim_assoc' IOVAR in the brcmfmac driver because the firmware no longer supports it, preventing error messages during roaming.
This commit adds a board configuration (adl_rt5682) to support the alc5682 headset codec on platforms without a speaker amplifier. It connects the codec to SSP0 following Intel's BT offload design.
The commit ensures that zerocopy sendmsg requests fail when the socket does not support them, similar to how previous patches handled zerocopy send requests.

The commit modifies the documentation build process to check for the presence of xeCJK.sty before loading it. This addresses an issue where certain distributions might not have this file installed, leading to errors during PDF generation.
The commit modifies checkpatch.pl to stop flagging certain 'case' macros as errors, allowing existing in-kernel driver code that uses these macros without issues.
The commit replaces simple-KMS helpers with atomic helpers in the DRM subsystem to improve code structure and facilitate sharing among drivers. No functional changes are made.
The commit introduces a JPEG decode worker interface to enable parallel operation of three hardware components on MT8195, enhancing media processing capabilities.
The commit removes VPRINTK() calls and the ata_scsi_dump_cdb() function in libata, as their information is already covered by tracepoints or can be retrieved from scsi tracing.

The commit adds support for entering zstate during extended vblank periods in FREESYNC_STATE_VIDEO. It calculates extra vblank time and allows zstate entry if the time exceeds 5ms, optimizing min_dst_y_next_start and restoring defaults upon exiting.
The commit fixes a type mismatch in the hqd_destroy() callback to prevent a Control Flow Integrity (CFI) failure when running on specific hardware with CONFIG_CFI_CLANG enabled.
The commit corrects the 'resets' property name in device tree bindings for the STM32 pinctrl driver. It also adds missing schema validation to prevent similar errors in the future.
The commit addresses a compiler warning related to array bounds in the ath9k wireless driver. It silences a warning where an array index of 32 is accessed beyond the array's size of 10.
The commit replaces pm_runtime_get_sync with pm_runtime_resume_and_get in the sun8i-ss crypto driver for better code simplicity.

The commit reorganizes code related to fixed file installation and removal in io_uring by moving it to the filetable section. It also modifies helper functions to use a context instead of an io_kiocb, improving code structure.

The commit updates the device tree bindings for the NXP i.MX8MQ VPU by splitting G1 and G2 decoder nodes into separate entities. This change allows each decoder block to be managed independently through the vpu-blk-ctrl power-domain controller, improving configuration flexibility.
The commit removes an unused macro (IWL_DEVICE_BZ_COMMON) and inlines its content into another macro. This simplifies the code by eliminating redundancy.
